doc-001.title: Q3 Retention Postmortem
doc-001.text: Net revenue retention fell to 104% in Q3, down from 119% in Q2. The decline traces almost entirely to the mid-market segment, where seat expansion stalled after the March pricing change. Enterprise NRR held at 128%. Churned mid-market accounts cited 'unclear ROI on the analytics add-on' in 61% of exit surveys.

doc-002.title: Analytics Add-on Usage Telemetry
doc-002.text: Only 22% of mid-market accounts that purchased the analytics add-on activated more than one dashboard in their first 90 days. Activated accounts renewed at 94%; non-activated accounts renewed at 58%. Time-to-first-dashboard is the single strongest predictor of add-on renewal in the model.

doc-003.title: March Pricing Change Rationale
doc-003.text: The March change moved the analytics add-on from a flat $200/mo to per-seat pricing at $18/seat/mo. The intent was to capture more value from large mid-market teams. Sales flagged at launch that small mid-market teams (under 12 seats) would see a price increase, which was deemed acceptable risk.
